skill.name: emailagentskill
skill.description: Plan, review, and coordinate agent-assisted email work with clear tool handoffs, QA checkpoints, approval gates, and safe execution boundaries.
skill.summary: Creates safe operating plans for agent-assisted email workflows.
skill.author: Sequenzy

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged eight weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but direct review found no cryptographic implementation or unsafe cipher use. The cited lines are descriptive email workflow guidance and YAML metadata, with no scripts, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection attempts found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 2 as weak cryptography. The line is a YAML short description for agent-safe email workflows and does not define or invoke a cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line was reviewed directly and contains descriptive metadata only. No evidence found of cipher, hash, secret, script, or execution behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 18 as weak cryptography. The line is a QA checklist item about review before execution and contains no cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line was reviewed directly and contains operational checklist prose only. No evidence found of weak algorithm use or code execution.

low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line was reviewed directly and is risk-gate documentation for live email systems. It does not implement hashing, encryption, or authentication logic.

low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line was reviewed directly and contains frontmatter prose only. No evidence found of cryptographic APIs, encoded payloads, or unsafe algorithms.

low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 8 as weak cryptography. The line gives workflow guidance about authority, evidence, and approval at handoffs.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 22 as weak cryptography. The line repeats workflow guidance about planning before tool use and explicit approvals.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line was reviewed directly and contains no executable content. No evidence found of cryptographic implementation or unsafe algorithm selection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 28 as weak cryptography. The line asks agents to describe provider-specific steps in operational language.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line was reviewed directly and is documentation text. No evidence found of cryptographic code, command execution, or hidden behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 48 as weak cryptography. The line identifies agent tools that may continue the work and does not include security-sensitive execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line was reviewed directly and only references supported agent tools. No evidence found of weak cryptography, exfiltration, or prompt injection.

content.value_statement: Email work can cross tools, data, approvals, and live systems. This skill structures agent handoffs, QA checks, and approval gates before execution.
actual_capabilities: Classifies agent roles for email planning, drafting, QA, analysis, and operations.
Defines read, draft, change, and execute permissions for each handoff.
Creates approval gates for live sends, imports, DNS changes, and production automations.
Structures tool handoff specs with inputs, outputs, acceptance criteria, and risk levels.
Produces audit-friendly status reports with assumptions, owners, risks, and next actions.
limitations: Does not send email or operate production email systems by itself.
Does not replace legal, compliance, deliverability, or brand review.
Requires source materials and permission boundaries to produce reliable plans.
Provider-specific instructions remain operational guidance, not live platform actions.

use_cases.title: Plan a Campaign Handoff
use_cases.description: Define what each agent should research, draft, review, and escalate before campaign execution.

use_cases.title: Review Email Automation Changes
use_cases.description: Create QA checkpoints and approval owners before agents edit templates, segments, or automation logic.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Multi-Agent Email Work
use_cases.description: Turn a complex email request into clear roles, handoffs, risk gates, and status updates.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Email Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for an agent-assisted email task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan an email workflow for [goal]. Identify agent roles, needed source materials, approval gates, and the next safe action.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Tool Handoff
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate setup for work that moves between agents or tools.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a handoff plan for [email task]. Include inputs, outputs, acceptance criteria, risk level, and human approval requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Production Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before editing a live email system.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to audit a proposed change to [template, segment, automation, DNS, or sending process]. Separate recommendations from actions and list rollback options.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate a Multi-Agent Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced coordination across planning, content, QA, and operations agents.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a multi-agent workflow for [email program]. Assign roles, permission boundaries, checkpoints, unresolved risks, and status handoff format.

output_examples.input: Plan a newsletter workflow that uses one research agent, one copy agent, and one reviewer.
output_examples.output: A role map, required inputs, draft and review handoffs, approval owner list, QA checklist, and next safe action.

output_examples.input: Audit a proposed import of a new customer segment into an email provider.
output_examples.output: A risk-gate review covering source data, consent status, suppression checks, approval owners, rollback plan, and execution boundary.

output_examples.input: Prepare a handoff for another agent to improve an abandoned-cart automation.
output_examples.output: A concise status report with assumptions, files reviewed, rejected options, remaining risks, acceptance criteria, and follow-up tasks.

best_practices: Provide source materials, platform context, permission boundaries, and known approvers before planning.
Separate recommendations from live actions whenever email sends, imports, DNS, or production automations are involved.
Ask for explicit checkpoints after segmentation, legal claims, performance interpretation, and production edits.
anti_patterns: Do not let an agent infer permission to send, import, delete, or edit production systems.
Do not skip QA because the request is framed as a small copy or template change.
Do not hide assumptions, missing consent data, unresolved risks, or rejected options from the final handoff.

faq.question: Does this skill send emails?
faq.answer: No. It plans and reviews workflows. It requires explicit approval before any live send or production action.

faq.question: Can it help with email compliance?
faq.answer: It can add review gates and owner assignments. It does not replace legal or compliance review.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. The source also describes handoffs for other AI agents.

faq.question: What inputs improve results?
faq.answer: Provide the goal, source materials, platform, permission boundaries, approvers, and production systems that could be affected.

faq.question: When should I use approval gates?
faq.answer: Use approval gates before live sends, imports, suppression edits, DNS changes, migrations, and production automation changes.

faq.question: Can another agent continue from its output?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill emphasizes clear handoffs, assumptions, source evidence, unresolved risks, and next actions.
